source_meaning: ballsiness, toughness, courage
target_meaning: a person with talent, courage, and outstanding acts, a hero

source_meaning: to sell someone a pup
target_meaning: to sell like hot cakes

source_meaning: dead of night (the middle of the night)
target_meaning: it is always darkest before the dawn

source_meaning: to besmirch the reputation of a person or thing
target_meaning: to tarnish; to besmirch

target_idiom: cáo chết ba năm quay đầu về núi
source_meaning: to be missed, to be needed
target_meaning: a person always longs for home

source_meaning: to wet one's whistle (to have drink)
target_meaning: toasting and drinking with each other

source_meaning: skin and bones (very skinny)
target_meaning: nothing but skin and bones; mere bag of bones

source_meaning: an extremely crowded, tight place or vehicle (a bus, a train, etc.), to the extent that the people are unable to move
target_meaning: packed like sardines

source_meaning: one by one; individually
target_meaning: on one's own; by oneself

source_meaning: to drown one's sorrows (drink alcohol heavily to deal with miserable feelings)
target_meaning: to share the joys and sorrows (usually emphasising the “sorrow” part)

source_meaning: separate the wheat from the chaff (to select only that which is of value)
target_meaning: separate the wheat from the chaff

target_idiom: gần chùa gọi bụt bằng anh
source_meaning: to humiliate, to play or to treat someone with despise
target_meaning: to treat somebody, especially a superior, overly casually due to familiarity

source_meaning: to become irritated
target_meaning: easily irritable

source_meaning: forcibly
target_meaning: to use force to coerce someone into submission is better than to use reason

source_meaning: to sweep something under the rug (to conceal a problem expediently)
target_meaning: to put a lot of effort into hiding something bad

source_meaning: flattering language to lure, that which deceitfully seduces, usually to lure into a trap
target_meaning: charming beauty who makes men fall and leads to disasters (such as a loss of wealth or reputation, or a toppled nation); dangerous woman; femme fatale

source_meaning: double-edged sword (benefit that is also a liability)
target_meaning: benefactors often get hatred from beneficiaries; no good deed goes unpunished

target_idiom: lấy dạ tiểu nhân đo lòng quân tử
source_meaning: to distrust; to doubt
target_meaning: to be cynical; to be skeptical of the integrity, sincerity, or motives of others; to psychologically project

source_meaning: expresses the belief that sometimes, or to some extent, it's normal or understandable succumbing to temptations
target_meaning: the spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak

source_meaning: an extremely chaotic situation
target_meaning: a situation where many people become homeless, especially after a disaster

source_meaning: to trick, delude, deceive or fool someone
target_meaning: deceiver, swindler, fraudster, scammer

source_meaning: in broad daylight (in a blatant and visible manner)
target_meaning: shameless; barefaced

source_meaning: to engage in a romantic relationship, especially marriage
target_meaning: to be well-matched in terms of socio-economic status (for purposes of marriage); to have similar family backgrounds

source_meaning: on the back burner (in low priority)
target_meaning: side job

source_meaning: to overcome or recover from a setback
target_meaning: to lapse back into one's old ways; to relapse into vice

source_idiom: sorriso de uma orelha a outra
source_meaning: smile from ear to ear (large smile)
target_meaning: a beautiful woman's smile is extremely precious

source_meaning: crocodile tears (display of tears that is forced or false)
target_meaning: crocodile tears

target_idiom: nước sông không phạm nước giếng
source_meaning: to mind one's own business (not to interfere in the affairs of others)
target_meaning: people should keep to themselves and mind their own business

source_meaning: to eat like a pig
target_meaning: gluttonous

source_meaning: the tendency of doing something because it is a habit even when it is not necessary or expected
target_meaning: habits and customs

source_meaning: to hang out to dry (to abandon someone in need)
target_meaning: to let unattended and expose to the elements

source_meaning: a mass (group of people) that is manipulated according to the interests of another, powerful group
target_meaning: having power or influence in society; powerful; influential

source_meaning: keep the momentum going; take the opportunity (to do something more); ride the wave.
target_meaning: to get wins after wins; to rake victories

source_meaning: good night; sleep tight (farewell said before going to sleep)
target_meaning: may you have a safe journey; bon voyage

source_meaning: for the love of God (expressing anger or exasperation)
target_meaning: Holy Mother of God!; Mother of mercy!; literally "Father and Mother!"

source_meaning: to take into account (to consider or regard)
target_meaning: to take something (usually a fraudulent claim) as a matter of fact without further inspection; to be too credulous

source_meaning: own goal (attitude against one's own interests)
target_meaning: when one's actions or results are objectively detrimental despite good intention

source_meaning: to take a long time to complete an action
target_meaning: overlong

source_meaning: head to toe
target_meaning: all the way

source_meaning: to reap what one sows (receive justice)
target_meaning: to reap what one sows

target_idiom: vắng chủ nhà, gà vọc niêu tôm
source_meaning: to make hay while the sun shines (to act while an opportunity exists)
target_meaning: when the cat's away the mice will play

source_meaning: to adopt certain ideas or attitudes that would make someone accepted by someone else or a group
target_meaning: chữ Hán form of nhập gia tùy tục/nhập gia tuỳ tục (“when in Rome, do as the Romans do”)
